e2e.summary: 12 passed / 6 failed across 6 viewports x 3 browsers (chromium, firefox, webkit). Failures isolated to chromium only due to SVG <circle> cx/cy parse errors (empty/NaN coordinates).

recommendations: Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers ~11KB
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers (saves ~150ms)

recommendations.action: Mobile path is shipping the same JS as desktop (R3F + three + drei + postprocessing). Move the dynamic `import('./_Scene')` INSIDE the `!isMobile && !isLowEnd` branch so the Scene chunk is never even prefetched on mobile. Currently next/dynamic is hoisted at module scope. Use a useEffect-driven import OR a conditional dynamic component. Expected mobile-bundle drop: ~150KB.

Chromium throws repeated `<circle> attribute cx/cy: Unexpected end of attribute. Expected length, "".` errors — failing all 6 chromium E2E tests. Find SVG <circle> elements where cx/cy values can be empty/undefined/NaN during initial render. Likely OrbitalBackdrop or PingRings. Default coordinates to 0 when unset; never let GSAP write empty strings. grep `cx=` and `cy=` for templates that use undefined refs.

Lighthouse mobile recommends 'reduce unused JavaScript ~154KB'. Check next.config.mjs and add `experimental: { optimizePackageImports: ['gsap', '@react-three/drei', '@react-three/fiber', 'three'] }` so Next prunes unused subpath exports. Also: explicitly import only the drei pieces actually used (e.g., `import { Float } from '@react-three/drei/web/Float'` rather than top-level).

Lighthouse mobile TBT 1572ms is a major blocker. The HeroWarrior + DevStation paths even when gated by `tier==='high'` may still execute their imports during initial parse. Wrap the entire HeroWarrior + DevStation modules behind `dynamic(() => import(), { ssr: false })` from the section component, so they're code-split out of the main route bundle.

Visual regression flagged the contact-section title wrapping awkwardly ('ING // THE_CO RE') at 375px and partially obscured by section badge '06' at 768px. Reduce title font size with clamp + adjust the section number badge z-index/position so it doesn't overlap. Verify with re-screenshot.

Visual regression overall score 45 is a FALSE NEGATIVE — caused by build corruption when 4 parallel agents ran `npm run start` against the same .next/ directory. Before the next iteration, do a clean `rm -rf .next && npm run build` and re-run visual regression in isolation (no parallel server starts). The actual layout is sound based on chromium-vs-firefox comparison.

Add Next.js compiler.removeConsole for production: `compiler: { removeConsole: { exclude: ['error', 'warn'] } }`. And add `swcMinify: true` (default in Next 14 but explicit). Also enable `productionBrowserSourceMaps: false` (default but verify) — sourcemaps in production add ~30% to served bundle.

issues: hero shows 'Application error: a client-side exception has occurred' - app crashes (major)
all other sections render as unstyled HTML fallback (no CSS, no canvas, no theme) (major)
missing ProjectVisuals canvas/svg core (major)
no HUD frame, no orange/dark theme applied (major)
hero renders blank white canvas with only grid lines - main visual missing (major)
about/skills/projects/experience/contact all show unstyled HTML fallback (major)
no CSS or canvas/svg core loaded (major)
complete loss of design system at desktop breakpoint (major)
hero renders blank white canvas with only grid lines - main visual missing (major)
all sections render as unstyled HTML fallback (major)
no CSS or canvas/svg core loaded (major)
complete loss of design system at large desktop breakpoint (major)
contact section title 'OPENING // THE_CORE' wraps awkwardly producing 'ING // THE_CO RE' (major text break)
contact section 'OPEN' prefix appears clipped above viewport when scrolled to anchor
contact title still wraps as 'ING // THE_COR E' due to large font on narrow column (major)
skills HUD bottom: 'LAT 37.77°N / LON -122.42°W' overlaps 'OPENAI API' text row (minor)
contact title 'OPENING // THE_CORE' partially obscured by '06' badge overlapping 'OPEN' (major)
experience section second card placeholder shows 'CORE // ONLINE' overlap with HUD frame (minor)

instructions: For each recommendation, identify the concrete file(s) to patch.

Common patches: code-split heavy imports with next/dynamic + ssr:false, defer non-critical JS with requestIdleCallback, swap large images for next/image with sizes/srcset, drop unused npm deps, add `<link rel="preconnect">` for fonts, tune Bloom intensity, adjust DPR cap, reduce Three geometry detail, replace expensive backdrop-filter with semi-opaque bg, hide HUD on small viewports.
After patching, run `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build`.
prompt.role: Senior frontend engineer applying iterative refinements
prompt.task: Apply each recommendation. Smallest viable patch per issue. Verify build after.
